summary: Waitlist protection features fully tested. All 15 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI verified - landing page waitlist form, login page, and dashboard all working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Waitlist check API correctly returns 'waitlist_reserved' when handle is reserved by different email
Waitlist check API correctly returns 'available: true, reserved_for_you: true' when email matches reservation
Handle claim API correctly rejects claims for handles reserved by different emails
Claim page passes user email when checking availability (line 46-47 in claim/page.tsx)
Rate limiting is aggressive (10 requests/60s) - may need adjustment for production
seed_data_creation: Test data: alice@example.com reserved 'alice' on waitlist, mockdev claimed by mock-user-dev
context_for_next_testing_agent: Waitlist protection features tested. Key APIs: /api/waitlist/check (with email param), /api/handle (POST validates against waitlist). Mock user email is dev@mock.local. Rate limiting is 10 req/60s per IP.

frontend_ui.landing_page: ✓ Waitlist form shows availability status (red X for reserved, green check for available)
frontend_ui.login_page: ✓ Dev Mode and GitHub OAuth buttons present
frontend_ui.dashboard: ✓ Shows @mockdev handle, verified badge, agent status

mocked_apis.note: Supabase Auth, Supabase Database, and GitHub OAuth are MOCKED via placeholder credentials
